reasoning: The BBB effective yield dropped to 4.91% on Feb 13, 2026, which is near its local minimum of 4.90% from October 2025. Corporate bond yields have historically exhibited strong resistance against falling below such levels due to credit spread floors and Treasury yield baselines. While short-term asset prices often approximate a random walk (suggesting a ~50% probability of increase), the recent sharp drop to a multi-month low creates a slight structural skew toward mean reversion (bounce back up), increasing the probability of a higher yield to slightly above 50% in the near term. The momentum is currently negative, which tempers this edge. Following forecasting principles, the probability for the closer date (Feb 22) reflects this mean-reversion expectation more strongly, while the probability for the more distant date (Mar 17) drifts closer to the 50% prior limit of a random walk.
